rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical three-component 1,2-aminosulfonylation of alkenes with sulfinates and amines to form 2-sulfonylethan-1-amines
electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of sulfinate (PhSO2Na) to sulfonyl radical; subsequent anodic oxidation of the benzylic C-radical adduct to a benzylic cation; cathode reduces protons to H2

key_intermediates.name: benzylic C-radical adduct B (after sulfonyl radical addition to alkene)
benzylic cation C (from anodic oxidation of B)

electron_flow: Anode oxidizes sulfinate to a sulfonyl radical which adds to the alkene giving a benzylic C-radical; this radical is further oxidized at the anode to a benzylic cation that is trapped by the amine nucleophile, while the cathode reduces protons to H2.

probe_reagents.name: TEMPO
probe_reagents.observation: Only trace 4aaa; >95% of 1a and 3a recovered — reaction suppressed, supporting radical pathway

probe_reagents.name: BHT (2,6-di-tert-butyl-4-methylphenol)
probe_reagents.observation: Yield of 4aaa dropped to 21%, consistent with radical intermediates

notes: Byproducts 5aa (vinyl sulfone via deprotonation of cation C) and 6aa (PhNHSO2Ph, from sulfonyl radical + amine) arise from high reactivity of intermediates A and B. Use of NH3 or NH3·H2O gave no product (4aan trace). Mechanism supported by CV (Figure S1, SI).
